Figure 1:The verification dose ceiling falls with delay.Critical doseβc=η​κmax\beta_{c}=\eta\kappa_{\max}versus verification delayδ\deltafor the binding modea=1a=1(blue) and
three lighter modes (grey); the loop is stable below each curve. The ceiling decreases monotonically
inδ\deltaand, atδ=2\delta=2, equals the inverse golden ratio(5−1)/2≈0.618(\sqrt{5}-1)/2\approx 0.618(red).
More verificationlatencytherefore forces a strictly smaller safe verificationstrength: the dose–delay tradeoff that the rest of the paper exploits.

Figure 2:Where to place correctors.(a) On three55-cliques chained by bridge edges, greedy
selection by the resolvent centrality (8) lowers the residual errortr⁡M​(R)−1\operatorname{tr}M(R)^{-1}faster
than degree-based or random placement (mean over300300orders), tracking the near-optimal frontier.
(b) Marginal centralityΔi\Delta_{i}per node; the first greedy picks (dark) are the high-leverage bridge
and hub nodes: the concrete answer towhere.

Figure 3:A second delay shrinks the safe region.Stability region (shaded) in the(p,q)=(η​μ,η​κ)(p,q)=(\eta\mu,\eta\kappa)plane for communication delayd=1d=1and verification delayδ=2\delta=2,
bounded by the oscillatory boundary (Theorem3, dashed) and theλ=−1\lambda=-1line
(red, here non-binding). The dose ceiling on theqq-axis is the same1/φ≈0.6181/\varphi\approx 0.618as in
Fig.1. Synchronizing the two delays (d=δd=\delta) collapses the region the most
(Corollary3).

Figure 4:Synthetic onset matches the predicted ceiling.(a) oscillation amplitude collapses
onto the predicted thresholdκ/κmax=1\kappa/\kappa_{\max}=1forδ=1,2,3\delta=1,2,3; (b) theδ=2\delta{=}2trajectory converges below the ceiling and oscillates above it.

Figure 5:Signed-error oscillation in a real Qwen3.6-35B numeric-estimation debate.Agents debate a quantity with a known true value under a delayed relative correction of graded gainα\alpha; the signed errorete_{t}can overshoot through zero. (a) Representative trajectories: the
stable cell (α=0.5,δ=1\alpha{=}0.5,\delta{=}1) decays to truth without overshoot, while the delayed cells
overshootthroughzero and oscillate: the Hopf signature, present in96%96\%of(α=0.5,δ=6)(\alpha{=}0.5,\delta{=}6)runs versus≤4%\leq 4\%atδ=1\delta{=}1. (b) Signed-error amplitude grows with
both delay and gain; the a-priori comparisonamp​(δ=6)>amp​(δ=1)\mathrm{amp}(\delta{=}6)>\mathrm{amp}(\delta{=}1)atα=0.5\alpha{=}0.5holds (8/88/8questions; the one-sided Wilcoxon hits itsn=8n{=}8floorp=0.004p{=}0.004). The only stable cell is
small-gain/small-delay, exactly the dose–delay region of the theory.
